title: edit-panel
content:
Edit Panel

The Edit panel provides tools for scene management, mesh editing, material adjustments, model rotation, and transform snapping.

Scene Operations

  • Clear scene. Keep docs + files – Removes all loaded 3D models from the scene. Documents (Docs panel) and attached files (Files panel) are preserved.

  • Reset whole model – Resets every object back to its initial position, rotation, and scale recorded at load time.

  • Cleanup (flatten unnamed nodes) – Simplifies the scene hierarchy by removing intermediate group nodes that have no name. Named objects and meshes remain intact.

  • Separate Mesh (split groups) – Splits the selected mesh into individual sub-meshes, one per geometry group. Requires a mesh with multiple material groups to be selected.

  • Merge Mesh (join descendants) – Merges all descendant meshes of the selected object (recursively, including nested groups) into a single mesh with material groups. Requires an object with at least one mesh descendant (inverse of Separate Mesh).

  • Remove numeric suffix (_1, _2…) – Strips trailing numeric suffixes (e.g. Part_1 → Part) from all object names. Useful for cleaning up names generated by CAD exporters.

  • Clear measurements – Permanently deletes all distance measurements, angle measurements, and CAD dimensions (flat and 3D) from the scene.

  • Clear annotations – Permanently deletes all annotations (flat and 3D) from the scene.

Transform Space

Transform: World space – Toggles the coordinate space used by the transform gizmo when moving, rotating, or scaling a selected object.

  • Checked (World) – Gizmo axes align with the global world coordinate system.

  • Unchecked (Local) – Gizmo axes align with the object's own local coordinate system.

Material Operations

CAD exporters sometimes produce models with legacy Three.js material types. These tools detect and upgrade them to MeshStandardMaterial, which supports physically-based rendering (PBR).

  • Check/Convert MeshBasicMaterial – Finds all objects using MeshBasicMaterial (unlit, no shading) and offers to convert them to MeshStandardMaterial.

  • Check/Convert MeshPhongMaterial – Finds all objects using MeshPhongMaterial (older specular shading) and offers to convert them to MeshStandardMaterial.

  • Check/Convert MeshLambertMaterial – Finds all objects using MeshLambertMaterial (diffuse-only shading) and offers to convert them to MeshStandardMaterial.

  • List All Material Types – Shows a summary dialog listing every material type present in the loaded models together with its count.

  • Check/Remove textures – Counts all textures (color, normal, roughness, metalness, etc.) across all materials. If any are found, offers to strip them all and free their GPU memory.

Whole Model Rotation

Rotates all loaded models together in 90° steps around a world axis. Use this to correct models that were exported with a different up-axis convention.

  • Rotate X +90° / −90° – Rotates all models around the X axis by +90° or −90°.

  • Rotate Y +90° / −90° – Rotates all models around the Y axis by +90° or −90°.

  • Rotate Z +90° / −90° – Rotates all models around the Z axis by +90° or −90°.

  • Bake rotation – Applies the current rotation permanently into the object geometry so the displayed rotation resets to (0°, 0°, 0°). Recommended before defining assembly workflows.

Snap

Controls the snapping behaviour of the transform gizmo. Hold Ctrl while dragging a gizmo handle to snap movement, rotation, or scale to the configured step size.

  • Mode – Sets the active gizmo mode: translate, rotate, or scale.

  • Translation – Snap step for translation in scene units (range 1–1000).

  • Rotation (°) – Snap step for rotation in degrees (range 1–90).

  • Scale – Snap step for scaling (range 0.01–2.00).
